title: Regular expressions should avoid containing multiple spaces
description: Regular expressions should not contain multiple spacesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEregextype-dependentWhy is this an issue?Multiple spaces in a regular expression can make it hard to tell how many spaces should be matched. It’s more readable to use only one space and then indicate with a quantifier how many spaces are expected.Noncompliant code exampleconst pattern = /Hello, world!/;Compliant solutionconst pattern = /Hello, {3}world!/;Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSourc
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Regular expressions should avoid containing multiple consecutive spaces - use \s+ or \s{n} instead
